summary: Good‑quality audience recording of a vibrant 1981 show with standout Scarlet Begonias/Fire on the Mountain and a strong first set, though the tape lacks bass and has thin keyboards.
review: The tapes capture an energetic 1981 performance where fans rave about the tight first‑set selections—Friend of the Devil, El Paso, Cumberland Blues, Music Never Stopped—and a killer second half featuring Scarlet Begonias, Fire on the Mountain and Black Peter. The audience source is generally clear, but listeners note missing Phil’s bass and a tinny Brent keyboard tone, while drums sit low in the mix.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the show’s song selection and energy (70% weight) and describe the audience recording as decent to good despite missing bass and thin keyboards (30% weight), yielding an overall rating just under 4 stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear enough for most of the jam, but lacks bass, drums are low, and keyboards sound tinny; a splice is noted on the companion SBD but not on this AUD.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Excellent tune selection with a vibrant first set and a second‑set that ramps up energy, highlighted by Scarlet/Fire and a strong Black Peter finish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Hypnotic, extended guitar solo praised; described as exciting and melodic.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass is largely absent in this audience recording; reviewers note the lack of Phil's low end.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent's six‑note lick noted as tinny; keyboards present but thin.
band_member_comments.Drums: Some drum presence audible but generally low and under‑represented.
